USD.catalyst: The U.S. dollar advanced to 162.48 yen on Monday, July 20, as intensifying conflict between the U.S. and Iran pushed Brent crude to $90 and spurred safe-haven demand. Sentiment is further supported by the appointment of Kevin Warsh as Federal Reserve Chair, whose hawkish leanings have driven up U.S. real rates and prompted global pension funds to pull back on FX hedges. Fed 3.75%. Markets are now looking toward upcoming growth data to guide Fed expectations following the "Liberation Day" market unrest of the previous year.

sources.title: Dollar firmer as US-Iran conflict intensifies, Brent hits $90 - CNBC
Global pension funds pull back on FX hedges as US dollar woes ease - Forex Factory
South Korea’s Kospi drops nearly 5% as some AI stocks swoon, while oil keeps climbing - AP News
South Korea's Kospi drops 4.5% as some AI stocks swoon, while oil keeps climbing - The Sun Chronicle

GBP.catalyst: Sterling strengthened to 1.3465 as UK GDP data released on July 16 exceeded expectations, prompting economists to upgrade growth forecasts despite previous weakness in business surveys. The currency is benefiting from perceived political stability as the UK awaits a new Prime Minister, providing a tailwind against a backdrop of broader dollar volatility. GBP/USD maintains a bullish technical profile, trading at 1.3454 and remaining above its 20-day, 50-day, and 200-day moving averages. Market participants are now focused on a data-packed week for the UK, which will test the resilience of the recent rebound against the Japanese yen and other peers.

sources.title: Pound Sterling: UK Growth Beats Expectations As Economists Upgrade GDP Outlook - Exchange Rates UK
Dollar drifts as US-Iran conflict intensifies, Brent hits $90 - Reuters

EUR.catalyst: The Euro remains under pressure as the European Central Bank prepares for its July 23 meeting, with policymakers citing the return of $90 oil prices as a primary concern for the inflation outlook. While the Q2 SAFE survey indicated that inflation and wage growth expectations are easing, Commerzbank analysts suggest a hawkish ECB tone may be required to limit downside against a firmer dollar. EUR/USD is currently trading at 1.144, holding above its 20-day moving average but remaining below its 50-day and 200-day moving averages. Investors are monitoring whether tightening loan conditions, as reported in the SAFE survey, will influence the Governing Council's policy trajectory this Thursday.

sources.title: Back to square one: Five questions for the ECB - Reuters
Dollar drifts as US-Iran conflict intensifies, Brent hits $90 - Reuters

JPY.catalyst: The Japanese yen weakened to 162.35 against the dollar, its lowest level since July 9, as the escalation of Middle East hostilities and rising oil prices weighed on the currency. A crowded carry trade remains the dominant theme, with the 275bp Fed-BoJ differential sustaining bids for USD/JPY despite the risk of intervention near the 160.00 level. JPY COT data shows leveraged funds holding a significant net short position of -105,238 contracts, leaving the pair vulnerable to a sharp unwind if the VIX exceeds 25. The pair is currently trading above its 20-day, 50-day, and 200-day moving averages as geopolitical tensions continue to favor the greenback.

sources.title: Dollar firmer as US-Iran conflict intensifies, Brent hits $90 - CNBC
World shares mostly decline with South Korea's Kospi down 6.4%, while oil prices slip - The Sun Chronicle

AUD.catalyst: AUD/USD is trading near 0.6984, supported by a recovery toward the 0.7000 handle despite persistent data misses reflected in a negative CESI reading of -13. The currency faces headwinds from a 4.5% drop in South Korea’s Kospi and a broader "risk-off" mood driven by the U.S.-Iran conflict, which has offset the RBA’s 60bp carry advantage over the Fed. Institutional hedging demand is elevated, with implied volatility at 12.1% creating a +5.7% volatility risk premium over realized levels. Technically, the pair sits above its 20-day and 200-day moving averages but remains capped below its 50-day moving average of 0.7051.

CAD.catalyst: The Canadian dollar is trading at 1.4018, showing a severe correlation breakdown with WTI oil as the traditional energy link hit a z-score of -1.91σ. Domestic sentiment is weighed down by uncertainty surrounding CUSMA negotiations and the impact of U.S. BoC 2.25%. The BoC recently held its key overnight rate at 2.25% in June, maintaining a 150bp disadvantage against the Fed's 3.75% rate. USD/CAD is currently positioned below its 20-day moving average but remains above its 50-day and 200-day moving averages.

sources.title: Canadian dollar forecasts cut amid CUSMA uncertainty
BoC Holds Rate at 2.25%: Canadian Dollar, Gold React | June 2026 - News and Statistics

CHF.catalyst: The Swiss Franc is seeing defensive flows as a zero-rate safe haven, though it remains caught between soft domestic data and a volatile geopolitical environment. Corporate activity is providing a specific catalyst, as South Korea's Samsung Biologics announced an $1.8 billion all-cash bid for the Swiss-listed PolyPeptide on July 20. USD/CHF is trading at 0.8069, which is below its 20-day moving average but above its 50-day and 200-day moving averages. Traders are monitoring whether the escalation in the Middle East will trigger a more sustained move into the franc as risk appetite remains fragile.

sources.title: Samsung Biologics to launch $1.8 billion all-cash bid for PolyPeptide - Reuters

NZD.catalyst: NZD/USD is trading at 0.5845, supported by a -0.58% real rate that is less accommodative than the Japanese yen, alongside speculation of a hawkish RBNZ delivery. With the RBNZ rate at 2.5% and COT long positioning at an extreme low of 15.2%, analysts identify a significant short-squeeze risk if upcoming data surprises to the upside. The market is specifically focused on the Q2 2026 CPI report as the next major catalyst for policy direction. The pair currently maintains a constructive technical stance, trading above its 20-day, 50-day, and 200-day moving averages.

sources.title: Dollar firmer as US-Iran conflict intensifies, Brent hits $90 - CNBC
New Zealand beats Ireland 40-21 to stay unbeaten in the Nations Championship - AP News

NOK.catalyst: The Norwegian Krone is trading at 9.637 against the U.S. dollar, positioned below its 20-day moving average but above its 50-day moving average. The primary domestic focus is the upcoming corporate earnings season, with Norsk Hydro scheduled to release its second-quarter 2026 results at 07:00 CEST on July 22. While rising oil prices typically support the krone, the currency remains sensitive to the broader risk-off sentiment affecting European assets. The market is currently range-bound as it awaits the July 22 webinar and presentation for further insight into the domestic industrial outlook.

sources.title: Norsk Hydro: Reminder - Invitation to Hydro's second quarter results 2026 - markets.businessinsider.com

SEK.catalyst: The Swedish Krona is trading at 9.645 against the dollar, remaining above its 50-day and 200-day moving averages despite being below the 20-day mark. Positive domestic corporate data provided a lift on July 17, as industrial technology group Karnell reported Q2 EBITA exceeding SEK 100 million and achieved its 15% margin target. However, the currency remains influenced by the broader European macro environment and the ECB's upcoming policy decisions. In the absence of fresh central bank catalysts from the Riksbank, the krona is tracking global risk sentiment and regional inflation expectations.

sources.title: Karnell Q2 2026 slides: EBITA tops SEK 100M, hits 15% margin target - Investing.com
